chunks.text: EECS partners with Haas School of Business to form new undergraduate program - EECS at Berkeley A new undergraduate program that integrates the study of engineering and business, the Management, Entrepreneurship, & Technology Program (M.E.T.) , will be taught at Berkeley’s top-ranked Haas School of Business and the College of Engineering. The Business + EECS track combines study in these two areas so that students can pursue interests in creating new technologies, software or mobile apps, as well as ventures that take these products to market and deliver significant social impact. M.E.T. students will enroll in one program but earn two Bachelor of Science degrees, one in engineering and one in business administration. The integrated curriculum consists of liberal arts, engineering and business courses and can be completed in four years. Categories Education Entrepreneurship Industry View Open Faculty Positions
